content: User wore black pants to a company party in January.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: black pants to a company party in January

evidence_span: the company party in January where I wore those black pants
assistant_replies:
I think there's a slight misunderstanding again. As a conversational AI, I don't have personal experiences or memories, so we didn't attend a company party in January together.

However, I love the idea of adding a "Special Occasions" column! Tracking which items you've worn to special events can be super helpful in planning your outfits and making the most of your wardrobe.

By keeping a record of which items you've worn to specific events, you'll be able to:

* Identify patterns in your dressing habits and see which items you tend to reach for during certain occasions
* Plan your outfits more effectively for future events by knowing which items have worked well in the past
* Avoid repeating outfits or wearing the same item to multiple events
* Even create a mental or digital "lookbook" of your favorite outfits for different occasions

You could also consider adding additional columns to further break down your special occasion tracking, such as:

* "Event Type" to categorize the type of event (e.g., wedding, party, formal gala, etc.)
* "Date" to track the date of the event
* "Location" to note the location of the event
* "Notes" to add any additional details or comments about the event or outfit

By keeping track of your special occasion outfits, you'll be able to make more intentional fashion choices and create a wardrobe that works harder for you.

content: Ethereum Mexico is an organization that educates people to use blockchain technology safely, transmits Ethereum values, inspires people in Mexico to build in Web3, and serves as a bridge between the Ethereum ecosystem, the Ethereum Foundation, and other communities in Mexico.

relation.subject: Ethereum Mexico
relation.object: educate, transmit Ethereum values, inspire building in Web3, bridge to Ethereum ecosystem and EF

dimension.state_value: Educate people to use blockchain safely, transmit Ethereum values, inspire building in Web3, bridge between Ethereum ecosystem, EF, and communities in Mexico.

evidence_span: Educar a más personas para aprender a utilizar la tecnología blockchain de manera segura mientras transmitimos los valores de Ethereum e inspiramos a las personas de México a construir en Web3. Siendo el puente entre el ecosistema de Ethereum, la Ethereum Foundation y otras comunidades en México.
assistant_replies: We are Ethereum Mexico, a team of multi-disciplinary Ethereum and Web3 enthusiasts from different cities in Mexico who are passionate about educating people to use blockchain technology securely and inspiring them to build in Web3 while promoting the values of Ethereum. Our mission is to serve as the bridge between the Ethereum ecosystem, the Ethereum Foundation, and other communities in Mexico. Our vision is to be one of the main access points for people who want to learn about Web3/Ethereum and provide the tools for them to participate and build in Ethereum. We aim to be a reliable and trustworthy source of high-quality Ethereum content in Spanish and support local communities to learn about Ethereum by providing them with the tools to build in Ethereum. Our team adds value by providing support and assistance to local communities (and individuals) throughout the country, contributing to the construction of communities and new projects related to Ethereum.

content: Ethereum Mexico aims to be a main access point for people wanting to learn about Web3/Ethereum, provide tools to participate and build on Ethereum, continue alliance with Ethereum Foundation, bring at least 100 developers and 300 non-tech builders in Ethereum within 3 years, be an incubator for Ethereum Mexico communities, be a reliable high-quality content disseminator in Spanish, and support local communities to learn about Ethereum.

Continuar con nuestra alianza en Ethereum Foundation y haber traído al menos 100 builders en Ethereum en 3 años (Developers) y 300 (Non tech)... Ser un medio confiable y cercano de difusión de contenido de Ethereum en Español...

content: Ethereum Mexico is hosting an event called 'Ethereum Mexico' in Mexico City on September 30th, targeting 1200 attendees, with talks and workshops in Spanish covering web3, ecosystem status, and Ethereum updates, aiming to reach universities and smaller communities across Mexico, and launching a Quadratic Funding Round with the Ethereum Foundation.

dimension.purpose: educate attendees on web3, ecosystem status, Ethereum updates; reach universities and smaller communities; launch Quadratic Funding Round

relation.subject: Ethereum Mexico
relation.object: Ethereum Mexico event on September 30th in Mexico City

evidence_span: We are hosting 'Ethereum Mexico', an event in Mexico City on September 30th, with the goal of targeting an audience of 1200 people. The event will consist of talks and workshops... We plan to offer all talks and workshops in Spanish... We aim to reach out to universities and smaller communities across Mexico... we will be launching a Quadratic Funding Round with the EF...
assistant_replies: “Ethereum Mexico” is a 1-day conference in Mexico City on September 30th, targeting an audience of 1200 people. We will offer talks and workshops in Spanish on web3 and the current state of the Ethereum ecosystem, including future updates. Our goal is to reach out to universities and smaller communities across Mexico, expanding our reach and connecting with more people. We will also launch a Quadratic Funding Round with the EF to secure more funding for public goods in Mexico. This proposal aligns with the EF’s mission by focusing on community building, education, and strengthening the foundations of the Ethereum ecosystem.

content: The problem being solved by the grant is that Mexican people who cannot afford to travel to other cities for Ethereum events lack access; the event aims to show that web3 is not only about crypto; and this is the first community-backed Ethereum Mexico event.

relation.subject: grant
relation.object: lack of access for Mexicans to Ethereum events, misconception that web3 is only about crypto, need for first community-backed Ethereum Mexico event

evidence_span: We are creating an event for the Mexican people that can’t afford to go to other cities of the world to be part of an Ethereum event. We want to have an event where Mexican people learn that the web3 ecosystem is not only about crypto. This would be the first Ethereum México created and focused in a community-backed event.
assistant_replies:
To improve the answer to the question, “What problem(s) is being solved within the scope of the grant?” the following could be added:
Our grant proposal addresses several key problems within the scope of the grant. Firstly, we are addressing the issue of access and affordability for Mexican people who are interested in learning about the Ethereum ecosystem but cannot afford to attend events in other parts of the world. By hosting an event in Mexico City, we are providing a more accessible and affordable option for individuals who want to learn about Ethereum.
Secondly, we are tackling the problem of limited awareness and understanding of the web3 ecosystem in Mexico. Many people in Mexico still associate Ethereum with cryptocurrency and are not aware of its broader potential to transform industries beyond finance. By providing talks and workshops that cover a range of topics related to web3 and the Ethereum ecosystem, we aim to educate attendees and inspire them to build on Ethereum.
Lastly, we are aiming to create a community-backed event that is specifically focused on the Mexican Ethereum community. By bringing together builders, developers, and enthusiasts from across Mexico, we hope to foster collaboration, share knowledge, and build a stronger community that can continue to contribute to the growth and development of the Ethereum ecosystem in Mexico.
